project.name: kloudvestigate
project.purpose: Internal telemetry investigation and publication-material preparation tool for KloudTrack station data.
project.framework: Next.js 16.2.6 App Router with React 19.2.4 and TypeScript.
project.tokenSavingSummary: Use this file as the compact project map for future model work. The app has server-rendered App Router pages, small client islands for dashboards and Mermaid rendering, route-handler APIs as the backend-for-frontend layer, server-only KloudTrack and Gemini calls, deterministic telemetry analysis before AI explanation, and demo data fallback when upstream tokens are missing.

client: app/page.tsx renders the main investigation dashboard shell.
app/pubmat/page.tsx renders the publication-material workflow shell.
components/telemetry/* contains client dashboards, forms, tables, markdown rendering, and charts.
components/architecture/MermaidDiagram.tsx is the browser-only Mermaid renderer used by the architecture page.
server: App Router pages are Server Components by default unless a child component declares use client.
app/api/investigations/route.ts handles investigation runs and optional copilot answers.
app/api/pubmat-quick-fetch/route.ts fetches station buckets for publication tables.
app/api/pubmat-chat/route.ts answers questions over the fetched publication table.
app/api/stations/route.ts returns station metadata from KloudTrack or demo data.

majorModules.name: Telemetry UI
majorModules.responsibility: Owns operator controls, quick fetch UX, fetched values tables, markdown answers, summaries, events, and visualization panels.

majorModules.name: KloudTrack API adapter
majorModules.responsibility: Keeps x-kloudtrack-key server-side, resolves metric history endpoints, fetches dashboard/history data, and normalizes upstream responses.

majorModules.name: Telemetry analysis
majorModules.responsibility: Computes deterministic summaries, interval buckets, gaps, duplicates, spikes, flatlines, threshold crossings, range violations, and metric metadata.

majorModules.name: AI context and providers
majorModules.responsibility: Builds compact JSON prompts, provides deterministic fallback explanations, and calls Gemini only from server routes.

security: assertInternalAccess checks INTERNAL_COPILOT_TOKEN in production-like environments.
KloudTrack and Gemini API keys are read only in server modules and route handlers.
writeAuditEvent currently emits structured audit JSON to console.
Production should run behind SSO or VPN and configure INTERNAL_COPILOT_TOKEN.
dataFlow: Operator selects station, metric, time range, interval, and question.
Client component POSTs to an App Router route handler.
Route handler validates internal access and request shape.
Server loads telemetry from KloudTrack or demo data.
Normalizer converts upstream payloads into typed station and telemetry records.
Deterministic analysis creates summaries and anomalies before any AI call.
AI context builder omits raw minute arrays and includes compact findings.
Gemini answers from structured context when configured; otherwise deterministic fallback is returned.
Client renders tables, charts, events, markdown, and status summaries.

diagrams.title: Server Architecture
diagrams.description: Route handlers form a backend-for-frontend layer around authentication, upstream data access, deterministic analysis, compact AI context, and provider calls.
diagrams.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  Client[Client dashboards] -->|GET/POST| Routes[Next App Router route handlers]
  Routes --> Auth[assertInternalAccess]
  Auth --> Loader[Telemetry loaders]
  Loader --> Demo[Demo telemetry fallback]
  Loader --> Kloud[KloudTrack API adapter]
  Kloud --> Normalize[Normalize station and telemetry records]
  Demo --> Normalize
  Normalize --> Analyze[Deterministic analysis]
  Analyze --> Context[Compact AI context]
  Context --> Gemini[Gemini provider]
  Context --> Fallback[Deterministic fallback]
  Gemini --> Response[JSON response]
  Fallback --> Response
  Analyze --> Audit[Structured audit log]

diagrams.title: Client Architecture
diagrams.description: Server pages keep shells light, while focused client components own stateful investigation, publication, markdown, table, and Mermaid interactions.
diagrams.mermaid:
flowchart TD
  Layout[app/layout.tsx] --> Home[app/page.tsx]
  Layout --> Pubmat[app/pubmat/page.tsx]
  Layout --> Architecture[app/architecture/page.tsx]
  Home --> Dashboard[TelemetryInvestigationDashboard]
  Pubmat --> Workspace[PubmatWorkspace]
  Workspace --> QuickFetch[PubmatQuickFetch]
  Workspace --> Chat[PubmatChatPanel]
  QuickFetch --> Values[FetchedValuesTable]
  Chat --> Markdown[MarkdownRenderer]
  Architecture --> Mermaid[MermaidDiagram client island]

diagrams.title: Token Strategy
diagrams.description: Raw telemetry stays out of prompts; the model receives a compressed, deterministic explanation surface.
diagrams.mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
  participant Operator
  participant Client
  participant API as Route Handler
  participant Analysis as Deterministic Analysis
  participant Context as AI Context Builder
  participant Model as Gemini or Fallback
  Operator->>Client: Ask investigation question
  Client->>API: Send selection and question
  API->>Analysis: Analyze normalized records
  Analysis->>Context: Summaries, events, intervals, warnings
  Context->>Model: Compact prompt without raw minute arrays
  Model-->>API: Answer grounded in supplied context
  API-->>Client: JSON answer and analysis payload

environment: KLOUDTRACK_API_TOKEN enables real upstream telemetry; absence uses demo data in current flows.
INTERNAL_COPILOT_TOKEN guards internal API access when configured or in production.
GEMINI_API_KEY enables AI answers; absence uses deterministic fallback text.
futureWork: Add automated tests around metric endpoint resolution, bucket windows, and prompt compaction.
